verdict.answer_first: B states the bottom line immediately: "Default to a monorepo for 3 related services"; A also answers first but softens it with "my default recommendation" and "it genuinely depends".
verdict.decisiveness: B commits cleaner to one recommendation: "Default to a monorepo" and "What I'd actually do: one repo"; A gives the same call but spends more space on option-survey framing.
verdict.fabrication: B invents scale color with "thousands of devs, giant histories"; A invents less and labels scale generally as "hundreds of projects". Neither claims fake measured data.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives the prescription compactly: "one repo, three top-level service directories plus a shared package, per-service deploy pipelines".
